sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: RTX Awarded $3.81B Contract for F-35 Jet Engines
feed.summary: RTX has been awarded a $3.81 billion contract modification for the production of F135 propulsion systems for the F-35 Joint Strike Fighter program. This contract finalizes Lot 18 engine production and includes additional F135 engines for Lot 19 F-35 aircraft, supporting the U.S. Air Force, Marine Corps, Navy, F-35 cooperative partners, and Foreign Military Sales customers. The F135 engine, developed by Pratt & Whitney, powers all F-35 variants, with an Engine Core Upgrade currently in development for future Block 4 configurations.

feed.title: Burford Capital (BUR) CFO gains shares via RSU, PSU vesting and tax withholding
feed.summary: Burford Capital's CFO, Jordan David Licht, recently gained 36,425 Ordinary Shares through the vesting of Restricted Share Units (RSUs) and Performance Share Units (PSUs), which were granted in 2023 and 2025. To cover tax obligations, 18,597 shares were withheld via net settlement at $7.70 per share. Following these transactions, Licht directly holds 68,350 Ordinary Shares, in addition to substantial outstanding RSU and PSU awards.

feed.title: STMicroelectronics Unveils GaN-Based Motor Control Reference Design
feed.summary: STMicroelectronics has introduced EVSTDRVG611MC, a new gallium nitride (GaN)-based reference design board for motor control in domestic appliances and industrial applications. This design leverages GaN's lower switching losses to reduce heat generation and eliminate the need for a heatsink in applications up to 600 watts, leading to more compact and cost-effective modules. The board integrates ST's STDRIVEG611 gate driver and an STM32G431 microcontroller, and is available for $125.06.

feed.title: Ciena Stockholders Elect Directors, Approve PwC Auditor and Say-on-Pay at 2026 Virtual AGM

feed.title: Aramco-Dow joint venture Sadara Chemical halts production over Middle East turmoil
feed.summary: Sadara Chemical Company, a joint venture between Saudi Aramco and Dow, has temporarily ceased production at its Jubail complex due to ongoing supply chain disruptions exacerbated by Middle East turmoil. The company, which has an annual production capacity of over 3 million metric tons of chemicals and plastics, announced that it cannot provide an estimate for the resumption of production, as it depends on domestic and international factors. This shutdown is expected to negatively impact Sadara's financial results for the current year, amid a broader regional conflict affecting energy supplies and the global economy.

feed.title: BJ's Wholesale Club Holdings: Downgrade To Hold For The Near Term (NYSE:BJ)
feed.summary: BJ's Wholesale Club Holdings (BJ) has been downgraded to a "hold" rating due to an anticipated shift in focus towards investments rather than earnings growth in FY2026. While the company demonstrates improving membership quality and strong performance from new club openings, near-term adjusted EPS growth is expected to slow to low-to-mid-single digits. This slowdown is attributed to normalized merchandise inventory (MFI) growth and increased selling, general, and administrative (SG&A) investments.

feed.title: Assessing WOTC and EZEC Eligibility for Professional Employer Organizations: A Review of Barrett Business Services, Inc. v. Commissioner
feed.summary: This article reviews the case of "Barrett Business Services, Inc. v. Commissioner," where the Tax Court ruled on the eligibility of Professional Employer Organizations (PEOs) for the Work Opportunity Tax Credit (WOTC) and the Empowerment Zone Employment Credit (EZEC). The court determined that only common law employers, not statutory employers or agents, are entitled to these income tax credits. Barrett Business Services, a PEO, was denied these credits because it did not supervise the day-to-day activities of worksite employees, failing to meet the common law employer definition.

feed.title: Jefferies downgrades Rockwell Automation stock rating on valuation
feed.summary: Jefferies downgraded Rockwell Automation (NYSE:ROK) from "Buy" to "Hold" and reduced its price target due to valuation concerns, despite the company's strong market position and potential for growth from industrial reshoring. Rockwell Automation is guiding for 4% organic growth in fiscal year 2026, with an expectation to reach its long-term 5% to 8% framework, supported by its dominant market share in programmable logic controllers in the U.S. The downgrade reflects premium valuations, as evidenced by a P/E ratio of 39.8 and a PEG ratio of 4.53, suggesting limited near-term upside.

feed.title: BlackRock stays No. 1 as Capital Group climbs in 2026 fund ranking
feed.summary: The 2026 U.S. Broadridge Fund Brand 50 report ranks BlackRock first for the second consecutive year, with Capital Group rising to second and Vanguard dropping to third. The report highlights that "Solidity," "Client-oriented thinking," and "Appealing investment strategy" are the most valued attributes by fund selectors. The study also noted a significant surge in active ETF launches (nearly 1,000 new funds) while mutual fund launches saw a 52% decline from 2024.

feed.title: BlackRock Retains Top Spot in the U.S. 2026 Broadridge Fund Brand 50 Report
feed.summary: BlackRock has maintained its position as the top asset management brand in the U.S. for the second consecutive year, according to Broadridge's 2026 Fund Brand 50 report. The report, which surveyed fund selectors' perceptions of brands based on ten attributes, highlighted "Solidity," "Client-oriented thinking," and "Appealing investment strategy" as the most valued attributes. Capital Group saw a significant rise to second place, while the study also noted a significant shift towards active ETF development over traditional mutual funds in 2025.

feed.title: The Best-Performing Fund Brands in Europe and Globally According to the 2026 Broadridge Fund Brand 50 Report
feed.summary: Broadridge's 2026 Fund Brand 50 (FB50) report reveals BlackRock maintains its top position globally and in most European markets, despite JPMorgan AM's brand score decline. The study highlights significant shifts in the asset management landscape, driven by geopolitical tensions, market volatility, and increasing investor interest in private equity, private debt, and innovative ETFs. Amundi, Schroders, and UBS also show notable brand strength improvements in the latest rankings.
